context.description: A German nonfiction book passage contrasts desperate flight with bodily exhaustion and survival limits. It argues that the body regulates access to energy reserves and that only a small, safe amount can be consciously used. The text frames deeper reserves as dangerous, costly, and not under ordinary conscious control.
normalization_notes: Continuous German philosophical-explanatory prose; treated as body chapter prose.
No explicit chapter or section heading visible on the page.

rows.paragraph_text: Desperate flight through a war zone, sustained exhaustion, and collapse at a stream before unconsciousness. The passage explains that only a small, safe portion of the body’s energy can be consciously used, while deeper reserves remain locked away because they are risky to access.

content.free_text: book_page book_body_content chapter_section German nonfiction prose on survival, exhaustion, bodily energy reserves, and the limits of conscious control over deeper biological resources.
